repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dist_synth_control/v1 Theorem 1: finite transport LP width W(d) for DTE tail probabilities after marginal identification

published_prior_art.cite: Russell2021: sharp bounds for joint-potential-outcome functionals with LP implementation; OberReynolds2023: OT endpoint values for joint-potential-outcome functionals

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ot_dte_marginal angle0: Choquet-OT path envelope and finite diagnostic G^eta_P(D) for pathwise DTE incompatibility

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019: finite-point Makarov regions are not uniformly sharp and can exclude stacked CDF vectors inside pointwise bounds

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ot_dte_marginal angle0 Conjecture 2: finite-support monotone-path diagnostic with positive gap

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019: gives finite-vector improvements and numerical illustrations, but not this nondegenerate marginal-overlap witness

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The finite LP/OT representation is a routine specialization of Russell2021 and OberReynolds2023, not a novel theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The main non-representability kernel is already the FirpoRidder2019 finite-vector nonuniform-sharpness phenomenon; G^eta_P(D) mainly metricizes that gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; to clear the flagship floor, the kernel needs a non-tautological extension beyond Firpo-Ridder, such as a closed-form or algorithmic zero-gap characterization for overlap envelopes.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: G^eta_P(D)=0 iff S^eta(P;D)=C^eta(P;D) follows from defining G as the full support-function gap plus standard compact-convex support separation; the positive-margin clause repeats Assumption face-conflict.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the zero-gap support-function iff with an independent characterization of when overlap-envelope endpoint faces intersect, stated as a computable condition or finite algorithm with input size and complexity.
Compute the proposed J=2 binary-by-ternary witness and include explicit primal tables or dual certificates showing G^eta_P(D) >= 0.05.

State a strict extension over Firpo-Ridder: identify an open class of non-singleton marginal-envelope laws where the support gap is positive and cannot be reduced to fixed-marginal finite-vector Makarov nonuniformity.
Add location anchors for the Frandsen-Lefgren and Lee consumer claims, separating consumer relevance from comparator novelty.

recommended_next_step: Revise around a genuinely new overlap-envelope characterization or algorithm; the current support-gap diagnostic is field-tier and below the flagship novelty floor.
